name: Harold Mabern
abstract: Harold Mabern Jr. (March 20, 1936 – September 17, 2019) was an American jazz pianist and composer, principally in the hard bop, post-bop, and soul jazz fields.

interviews.title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Benny Goodman Salute to Jazz Interactions
Kool Jazz Festival: Now's The Time

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: got a quartet at the time with Frank Strozier and Harold Mabern
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Eric Alexander
about_quotes.evidence: the first instructor I had in the small group ensemble was Harold Mabern... Playing with Harold? I mean if you don’t bring your A game and really step up, he going over, it’s like a tsunami.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eric Alexander interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: And I went up to play and it was with Jon Hendricks, the singer, who just happened to be there. And there was a great bass player, Jamil Nasser and Harold Mabern, a great pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: I was in the last part of the band, where it was Ron Carter, George Coleman, Harold Mabern, and an alto player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: It's a pleasure playing with him. These are people I had the pleasure of playing with

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Simmons
about_quotes.evidence: all those guys that came up from Memphis like George Coburn and all and Harold Mabern. They almost feel like they’re from Chicago because when they arrived, something happened in their musical experience.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norman Simmons interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
